name: Grape Tartlets with Almond Crust
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Butter (cold, unsalted), cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Finely grind almonds in a food processor with on/off motion or crush with a rolling pin until finely ground.
2. Add flour, salt, and brown sugar to the food processor and blend, or combine them in a bowl and stir to blend.
3. Add butter to the mixture and process or work with fingertips until it forms coarse crumbs.
4. In a small bowl, beat the egg lightly. Stir in the almond extract and lemon zest.
5. With the food processor on, add the egg mixture to the dough through the feed tube and process until the dough forms.
6. Pat the dough into a ball, wrap in plastic wrap, and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
7. Remove the dough from the refrigerator and divide it into 8 equal parts. Press each piece into a lightly buttered tartlet pan (3-3 1/2 inches), patting into an even layer. Chill the tartlet shells in the freezer for at least 30 minutes before baking.
8. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
9. Remove the tartlet shells from the freezer, line each with a square of foil, and fill with pie weights or dried beans to prevent the dough from puffing during baking.
10. Bake on a baking sheet in the center of the oven for 10 minutes.
11. Remove the foil and weights, reduce the oven temperature to 350°F, and continue baking for 15-20 minutes, or until the pastry is a rich, golden brown. Let the tartlet shells cool in the pans and place them on wire racks to cool completely.
12. To prepare the glaze, combine apricot preserves and sherry in a small saucepan and warm over medium heat, stirring, until the preserves are melted. Strain through a sieve.
13. To assemble tarts, brush the bottom of each tartlet shell with the glaze. Arrange grapes (cut sides down) in a decorative pattern, alternating colors, in the bottom of each pastry shell. Brush the remaining glaze over the grapes and edges of the tartlets.
14. Sprinkle toasted almonds over the tartlets before the glaze sets. Serve at room temperature.
